**Root cause:** my 19:38 awk had `print "      - GH_TOKEN=\${GH_TOKEN:-}"`. PowerShell heredoc converted backtick-`${...}` to literal `${...}` for bash correctly, but awk's string parser choked on the `}` (treats `}` as block-terminator under unbalanced quoting). Three-layer escape chain (PowerShell → bash heredoc → awk) had one layer too many for the assumed pass-through. Same class as P1.1/P1.2a/P3.6 — Sub-shape A inside my own dispatch text, n=4 within this dispatch arc.

Replaced `print "...${GH_TOKEN:-}"` with `printf "      - GH_TOKEN=%s%s\n", "$", "{GH_TOKEN:-}"`. awk sees `"$"` and `"{GH_TOKEN:-}"` as two distinct parser-safe literals; printf joins them in output; resulting compose-yml line is the verbatim `      - GH_TOKEN=${GH_TOKEN:-}` we want.
